rules: [P1] Apply protocol thresholds, including exact cutoff boundaries for borderline values, to retrieved results before ordering treatment, repeat testing, or other interventions.
[P1] When a protocol defines a medication/order family or trigger set, query the full qualifying set rather than a handpicked subset before concluding presence, absence, or no action.
[P1] When protocol actions depend on a primary trigger condition, verify that trigger first; if it is not met, do not pursue dependent companion-test or follow-on actions.
[P1] State explicitly when any requested item has no available result.

[P1] For observations such as vitals, check all qualifying representations—including standalone observations, panel/component measurements, and synonymous codes/names—before concluding no data are available.
[P1] Search prior procedures/studies using the exact requested type plus known code/name variants before concluding none exist.
[P1] When a protocol explicitly treats absence of qualifying prior documentation as due/eligible, state that no qualifying history was found and proceed with the instructed order/action.

[P1] For immunization- or vaccine-based decisions, review immunization history across all relevant record types first, match the exact vaccine type/family, and use the most recent qualifying administration date against the task reference date when assessing eligibility, prior doses, or recency.
[P1] For multi-item requests, retrieve the most recent qualifying result for each requested item independently.

[P1] For nested time-window aggregation requests, retrieve the full largest requested qualifying window once, verify timestamp boundaries and units, then derive each smaller window’s exact matching set and compute each aggregate independently.

[P1] For device/procedure removal or dwell-time actions, confirm the device is currently active/in place from active device documentation (not procedure history alone), distinguish completed insertion procedures from active placement episodes, use the current active placement episode’s original insertion timestamp for timing, confirm no documented removal/discontinuation already exists, and do not request removal if the elapsed-time threshold is not met.
[P1] If retrieved results do not meet intervention criteria, explicitly state that no protocol action is needed.
[P1] If qualifying data exist, return the required aggregate/result instead of a no-data message.

[P1] For recency-, elapsed-time-, or age-threshold actions, use the most recent qualifying matching event/documentation, anchor comparison to the task’s effective/current reference date, and if the threshold is unmet or unverifiable, do not act.
[P1] If required protocol inputs, prerequisite documentation/state, or supporting/companion results are missing, state they are unavailable and take no protocol action.

[P1] For order deduplication workflows, start with a targeted patient-scoped search of the full qualifying active order category, determine the zero/one/multiple branch only after complete retrieval, place a new qualifying order if none exist, leave a single qualifying active order in place, and if multiple exist, keep the newest qualifying active order and discontinue older duplicates.
[P1] If an initial retrieval is empty or appears incomplete for the task, validate with an alternate patient-scoped query/path before concluding absence or that no action is needed.
[P1] When a protocol depends on multiple related results, interpret the required results together and select the action from the combined protocol branch rather than any single value alone.
[P1] Complete the requested state change when supported, not just the audit; if one API path lacks the needed action, check for another supported workflow before concluding the update cannot be done.
[P1] Check canonical chart sources broadly for the clinical domain; use standard synonyms/codes and avoid relying on a single code/pathway.
[P1] Verify the exact requested item/order-to-code/type mapping before retrieving, aggregating, or ordering data; do not use related proxies without explicit mapping.
[P1] When placing imaging orders, preserve the requested modality/body region/contrast and indication exactly.

[P1] For paired-medication/order checks, query the complete active qualifying trigger set using correct status filters and family/category coverage when protocol-defined, verify required companion coverage for each qualifying trigger, and create the companion order when any qualifying trigger lacks coverage.
[P1] Count or act only on data/orders that meet the task’s exact qualification criteria, including status, setting, modality/body region, result pattern, and threshold branch as applicable.
[P1] Start with targeted patient-scoped queries for the relevant resource type and qualifying filters/status; avoid broad or irrelevant paginated browsing once the needed resource set is identified.
